title: Florentine Catasto of 1427 | The Online Cultural and Historical ...
highlights: In 1427, the Florentine government conducted an extensive and detailed account of the financial state of its citizens in what would be the first comprehensive modern tax assessment in Europe. Known as the Catasto, this archive represents the property holdings and net worth of every household in the city’s jurisdiction. The massive amount of data continued within the Catasto’s registers has provided the basis for a wide range of scholarly investigations. In the late 19th century it was used to excavate, document, and map the landholding patterns of the city’s central region by Guido  ...    publication of the monumental volume Les Toscans et leurs familles: une étude du “catasto” florentin de 1427. Tuscans and Their Families is a detailed analysis of the Florentine catasto of 1427, providing insights into the demographic structure, family arrangements, and wealth distribution in Renaissance Florence.
